p.strong: Complete Signal Delivery Architecture — User Space ↔ Kernel Space

g.text: ═══ Ring 3 → Ring 0 ═══ INT 0x30 (syscall) / INT 14 (page fault) / IRQ 1 (keyboard) ═══
KERNEL — Signal Dispatch Engine (runs BEFORE set_pdir_base)
KERNEL — Signal Delivery Engine (User Handler Path)
═══ Ring 0 → Ring 3 ═══ IRET (trap return) ═══ CPU loads EIP/ESP/EFLAGS from trap frame ═══

ul.li: Used by shell to forward SIGKILL / SIGINT to child

ol.li: CPU saves SS, ESP, EFLAGS, CS, EIP → kernel stack

ul.li: Set atomically, checked at trap return

p.strong: Called in trap() before switching to user page table

ul.li: != NULL → user registered a handler → deliver_signal()

div.p: Queue guard: only remove if READY Self-check: only call exit if self
Rewrites trap frame to redirect to user handler:

pre.code: 1. user_esp = tf->esp 2. Write trampoline at user_esp: B8 1C 00 00 00 MOV EAX, 28 (SYS_SIGRETURN) CD 30 INT 0x30 EB FE JMP -2 (safety spin) 3. Push: trampoline_addr, signum, saved_esp, saved_eip 4. tf->eip = sa->sa_handler 5. tf->esp = new_esp
saved_eip = *(user_esp + 4) saved_esp = *(user_esp + 8) tf->eip = saved_eip tf->esp = saved_esp

ul.li: Process resumes where it was interrupted
